section_anchor: The product ladder that captured every entry point
brief_summary: A three-tier vertical ladder diagram showing Keychron's K / V / Q series product progression. Each tier is a coral-outlined card with price range, key specs, and audience label. A buyer-progression arrow on the left edge runs bottom to top. The diagram grounds the article's argument that a buyer can ascend the entire product ladder without switching brands.
data_sources: apps/web/src/content/articles/keychron-quiet-takeover.mdx:The product ladder that captured every entry point — 'K series launched as a budget Bluetooth board. Hot-swap, ABS keycaps, accessible price'

apps/web/src/content/articles/keychron-quiet-takeover.mdx:The product ladder that captured every entry point — 'V series arrived as the mid-tier answer: QMK and VIA support, PBT keycaps, gasket mount at a sub-$100 price'

apps/web/src/content/articles/keychron-quiet-takeover.mdx:The product ladder that captured every entry point — 'Q series sits at the top of the stack — aluminum case, gasket mount, the same QMK/VIA firmware stack, better stock switches' and '$100–$180'
